query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Access', 'To gain or obtain access to', 'The author hoped the new policy would access tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accession', 'To make a record of (additions to a collection)', 'Over time, the community learned to accession its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accessory', 'Aiding in a secondary way, being additional,', 'Her accessory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acclaim', 'To praise enthusiastically', '“The critic acclaimed the novel as a masterpiece.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acclimate', 'To adjust to new conditions', 'The author hoped the new policy would acclimate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acclivity', 'A slope or inclination of the earth, as the side of a hill', 'Scholars noted a striking acclivity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accolade', 'An award or special recognition', '“She received an accolade for her outstanding performance.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accommodate', 'To adapt, as, to accommodate ourselves to circumstances', 'The author hoped the new policy would accommodate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accompaniment', 'That which gives support or adds to the background', 'Critics argued that the plan might accompaniment the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accompany', 'To perform an accompanying part or parts in a composition', 'The author hoped the new policy would accompany tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accomplice', 'A person who helps commit a crime', 'Her accomplice became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accomplish', 'To finish successfully', 'The author hoped the new policy would accomplish tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accord', 'To make to agree or correspond, to suit one thing to another', 'Over time, the community learned to accord its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accordion', 'To fold up, in the manner of an accordion', 'The author hoped the new policy would accordion tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accost', 'To approach and speak to someone boldly', 'The author hoped the new policy would accost tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Account', 'To compute, to count', 'Critics argued that the plan might account the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accouter', 'To furnish with dress or equipments', 'The author hoped the new policy would accouter tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accouterment', 'Dress, trappings, equipment', 'The reviewer described the tone as accouterment, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accredit', 'To put or bring into credit, to invest with credit', 'The author hoped the new policy would accredit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accrete', 'To grow or increase by gradual addition', 'Over time, the community learned to accrete its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accretion', 'Growth by slow buildup', 'The essay highlights the accretion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accrue', 'To accumulate over time', 'Over time, the community learned to accrue its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acculturation', 'Adopting cultural elements of another culture', 'Her acculturation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accumulate', 'To gather or collect something gradually over time', 'The author hoped the new policy would accumulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accurse', 'To devote to destruction', 'Critics argued that the plan might accurse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accusation', 'The act of  with a crime or with a lighter offense', 'The essay highlights the accusation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accuse', 'To find fault with, to blame', 'Over time, the community learned to accuse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Accustom', 'To make familiar by use, to cause to accept', 'The author hoped the new policy would accustom tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acerbic', 'Sharp and forthright, especially in speech or manner', 'Her acerbic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acetate', 'Any salt or ester of acetic acid', 'Critics argued that the plan might acetate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acetic', 'Of, pertaining to, or producing vinegar', 'Her acetic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Achillean', 'Invincible', 'Scholars noted a striking achillean between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Achromatic', 'Without colour', 'Scholars noted a striking achromatic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acidulous', 'Slightly sour, sub-acid', 'The reviewer described the tone as acidulous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acknowledge', 'To admit the knowledge of, to recognize as a fact or truth', 'The author hoped the new policy would acknowledge tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acknowledgment', 'The act of acknowledging, admission', 'Her acknowledgment became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acme', 'The top or highest point, pinnacle, culmination', 'Her acme became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acoustic', 'Relating to sound or hearing', '“The concert hall has excellent acoustic properties.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acquaint', 'To make familiar', 'Over time, the community learned to acquaint its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acquiesce', 'To accept or comply without protest', '“She acquiesced to her supervisor’s request.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acquire', 'To gain or obtain', '“He acquired a new skill through practice.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acquisitive', 'Acquired', 'The passage presents a acquisitive portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acquit', 'To declare or find not guilty, innocent', 'The author hoped the new policy would acquit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acreage', 'Size, as measured in acres', 'Her acreage became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acrid', 'Sharp and harsh, or bitter and not to the taste, pungent', 'The author hoped the new policy would acrid tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acrimonious', 'Angry and bitter in speech or tone', 'What seemed acrimonious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acrimony', 'Bitterness in manner or speech', 'Scholars noted a striking acrimony between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Acrophobia', 'Fear of heights', 'Scholars noted a striking acrophobia between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Actionable', 'Affording grounds for legal action', 'What seemed actionable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Actuality', 'The state of existing, existence', 'The essay highlights the actuality of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
